title: review pass: lib.rs headers of all crates
description: include links to project home and online docs

title: workflow: replace github action versions with sha
basil-nats: make crate no_std + alloc compatible
description: Investigation (2026-07-05) found basil-nats is a pure protocol/crypto crate (NATS ed25519-nkey JWTs, NKey encode/decode, xkey seal/verify) with zero async/tokio/socket/file/thread/env usage — all NATS transport already lives in the separate basil-nats-bridge crate. Workspace crypto deps (ed25519-dalek, x25519-dalek, sha2, zeroize) are already pinned default-features=false / alloc-only, so no_std+alloc is reachable with only two localized fixes (tracked as child issues): the std::collections::BTreeMap import and the thread_rng() call in seal_nats_curve. No existing no_std attempt or stated design goal in CHANGELOG/docs — this is net-new scope, not documented intent. Umbrella issue: add #![no_std] + extern crate alloc to src/lib.rs, add std/alloc cargo features gating optional std-only conveniences, and verify a no_std+alloc build once child issues land.

title: basil-nats: replace std::collections::BTreeMap with alloc equivalent
description: src/lib.rs:37 imports std::collections::BTreeMap. Swap to alloc::collections::BTreeMap (requires extern crate alloc once #![no_std] lands in the parent issue). Trivial, mechanical change — no behavior difference.

title: basil-nats: remove thread_rng() dependency from seal_nats_curve
description: src/lib.rs:354, inside seal_nats_curve, calls rand::thread_rng().fill_bytes(&mut nonce) — the one genuine std/getrandom blocker in the crate (everything else, including time validation via a caller-supplied now_unix: u64, is already OS-independent). Fix: change seal_nats_curve to accept an impl RngCore parameter (the RngCore trait is already imported/used in this file) instead of calling thread_rng() internally, and update call sites to pass a rng (e.g. thread_rng() at call sites that still have std, or a caller-chosen RNG under no_std).
